# terrible_community_general_oneandone_monitoring_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `add_ports` (String) Ports to add to the monitoring policy.
- `add_processes` (String) Processes to add to the monitoring policy.
- `add_servers` (String) Servers to add to the monitoring policy.
- `agent` (String) Set true for using agent.
- `api_url` (String) Custom API URL. Overrides the `ONEANDONE_API_URL` environment variable.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auth_token` (String) Authenticating API token provided by 1&1.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Monitoring policy description. maxLength=256.
- `email` (String) User's email. maxLength=128.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `monitoring_policy` (String) The identifier (id or name) of the monitoring policy used with update state.
- `name` (String) Monitoring policy name used with present state. Used as identifier (id or name) when used with absent state. maxLength=128.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `ports` (String) Array of ports that are to be monitored.
- `processes` (String) Array of processes that are to be monitored.
- `remove_ports` (String) Ports to remove from the monitoring policy.
- `remove_processes` (String) Processes to remove from the monitoring policy.
- `remove_servers` (String) Servers to remove from the monitoring policy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Define a monitoring policy's state to create, remove, update.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `thresholds` (String) Monitoring policy thresholds. Each of the suboptions have warning and critical, which both have alert and value suboptions. Warning is used to set limits for warning alerts, critical is used to set critical alerts. alert enables alert, and value is used to advise when the value is exceeded.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `update_ports` (String) Ports to be updated on the monitoring policy.
- `update_processes` (String) Processes to be updated on the monitoring policy.
- `wait` (Boolean) Wait for the instance to be in state 'running' before returning.
- `wait_interval` (Number) Defines the number of seconds to wait when using the _wait_for methods.
- `wait_timeout` (Number) How long before wait gives up, in seconds.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
